confidence_calculation.notes: High confidence based on documented GCP security advisories, public breach disclosures, and validated exploitation techniques in bug bounty programs.

owasp_top10: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures
mitre_attack: T1548.001 - Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism: Setuid and Setgid
T1069.003 - Permission Groups Discovery: Cloud Groups
cwe: CWE-732 - Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource

threat.title: Google Cloud Functions Service Account Privilege Escalation
threat.description: Cloud Functions, when deployed with the default Compute Engine service account or overly permissive custom service accounts, enable attackers to escalate privileges from function execution to project-wide administrative access. The default service account (PROJECT_NUMBER-compute@developer.gserviceaccount.com) is granted the Editor role (roles/editor) by default, providing broad permissions across the entire GCP project. Attackers exploiting code injection, SSRF, or deployment control can leverage cloudfunctions.functions.setIamPolicy, Secret Manager access, or service account impersonation to gain persistent access to sensitive resources including Cloud Storage, BigQuery, and Cloud SQL databases.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker gains initial access to Cloud Function via code injection vulnerability (e.g., command injection in Node.js function)
2. Enumerates service account identity using metadata service: curl -H 'Metadata-Flavor: Google' http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/email
3. Discovers function is running as default compute service account with Editor role (roles/editor)
4. Extracts service account access token: curl -H 'Metadata-Flavor: Google' http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/token
5. Uses cloudfunctions.functions.setIamPolicy permission to bind new malicious service account as invoker or grant themselves cloudfunctions.admin role
6. Deploys backdoored function with cloudfunctions.functions.create permission or accesses Secret Manager (secretmanager.versions.access) to exfiltrate database credentials, API keys, and other project secrets
7. Uses stolen credentials to pivot to Cloud Storage buckets, BigQuery datasets, or establishes persistence via service account key creation (iam.serviceAccountKeys.create)
8. Escalates to Project Owner by modifying IAM policies (resourcemanager.projects.setIamPolicy) if service account has sufficient permissions
prerequisites: Cloud Function deployed with default Compute Engine service account
Service account has Editor role (roles/editor) or equivalent broad permissions
Function contains exploitable vulnerability (code injection, SSRF, deserialization)
No VPC Service Controls or Organization Policy constraints enforced
No workload identity federation or short-lived credentials in use
Metadata service accessible from function runtime (default configuration)

security_research.title: GCP Privilege Escalation: Methods and Mitigations

security_research.title: Google Cloud Functions Security Best Practices
security_research.key_finding: Recommended mandatory use of user-managed service accounts with least-privilege permissions instead of default compute service account

mitigations.title: Implement Least-Privilege User-Managed Service Accounts
mitigations.description: Replace default Compute Engine service account with custom user-managed service accounts that have only the minimum required permissions for each specific function. Use principle of least privilege by granting only specific IAM roles needed for function operation.

code_example.code:
#!/bin/bash
# Create dedicated service account for Cloud Function
PROJECT_ID="your-project-id"
FUNCTION_NAME="process-orders"
SA_NAME="fn-process-orders"
SA_EMAIL="${SA_NAME}@${PROJECT_ID}.iam.gserviceaccount.com"

# Step 1: Create user-managed service account
gcloud iam service-accounts create ${SA_NAME} \
  --display-name="Service Account for ${FUNCTION_NAME} Function" \
  --description="Least-privilege SA with only Cloud Storage read access" \
  --project=${PROJECT_ID}

# Step 2: Grant ONLY required permissions (example: read from specific bucket)
gcloud projects add-iam-policy-binding ${PROJECT_ID} \
  --member="serviceAccount:${SA_EMAIL}" \
  --role="roles/storage.objectViewer" \
  --condition="expression=resource.name.startsWith('projects/_/buckets/orders-input-bucket'),title=Restrict to specific bucket"

# Step 3: Grant Cloud Logging permission for function logs
gcloud projects add-iam-policy-binding ${PROJECT_ID} \
  --member="serviceAccount:${SA_EMAIL}" \
  --role="roles/logging.logWriter"

# Step 4: Deploy function with custom service account
gcloud functions deploy ${FUNCTION_NAME} \
  --gen2 \
  --runtime=nodejs20 \
  --region=us-central1 \
  --source=. \
  --entry-point=processOrder \
  --trigger-http \
  --service-account=${SA_EMAIL} \
  --no-allow-unauthenticated

# Terraform equivalent:
# terraform/main.tf
# resource "google_service_account" "function_sa" {
#   account_id   = "fn-process-orders"
#   display_name = "Service Account for process-orders Function"
#   project      = var.project_id
# }
#
# resource "google_project_iam_member" "function_storage_viewer" {
#   project = var.project_id
#   role    = "roles/storage.objectViewer"
#   member  = "serviceAccount:${google_service_account.function_sa.email}"
#   
#   condition {
#     title       = "Restrict to specific bucket"
#     description = "Only allow access to orders-input-bucket"
#     expression  = "resource.name.startsWith('projects/_/buckets/orders-input-bucket')"
#   }
# }
#
# resource "google_cloudfunctions2_function" "function" {
#   name        = "process-orders"
#   location    = "us-central1"
#   description = "Processes incoming orders"
#   
#   build_config {
#     runtime     = "nodejs20"
#     entry_point = "processOrder"
#     source {
#       storage_source {
#         bucket = google_storage_bucket.source.name
#         object = google_storage_bucket_object.source.name
#       }
#     }
#   }
#   
#   service_config {
#     service_account_email = google_service_account.function_sa.email
#     ingress_settings      = "ALLOW_INTERNAL_ONLY"
#     available_memory      = "256M"
#     timeout_seconds       = 60
#   }
# }

# Verify service account permissions
echo "\n=== Service Account Permissions ==="
gcloud projects get-iam-policy ${PROJECT_ID} \
  --flatten="bindings[].members" \
  --filter="bindings.members:serviceAccount:${SA_EMAIL}" \
  --format="table(bindings.role)"

iso27001_controls: A.9.2.3 - Management of privileged access rights
nist_csf: PR.AC-1 - Identities and credentials are managed
validation_steps: Verify function is NOT using default compute service account: gcloud functions describe FUNCTION_NAME --format='value(serviceAccountEmail)'
Confirm service account has NO Editor/Owner roles: gcloud projects get-iam-policy PROJECT_ID
Test function operation with new service account to ensure required permissions are granted
Validate IAM conditions are properly restricting resource access
Review Cloud Audit Logs for unauthorized permission grants: gcloud logging read 'protoPayload.methodName="SetIamPolicy"'

mitigations.title: Implement VPC Service Controls and Organization Policy Constraints
mitigations.description: Use VPC Service Controls to create security perimeters around Cloud Functions and enforce Organization Policy constraints to prevent privilege escalation paths. Block metadata service access and enforce service account restrictions.

code_example.code:
#!/bin/bash
# Configure VPC Service Controls for Cloud Functions
PROJECT_ID="your-project-id"
ORG_ID="123456789012"
PERIMETER_NAME="cloud-functions-perimeter"

# Step 1: Create Access Context Manager policy
POLICY_NAME="default-policy"
gcloud access-context-manager policies create \
  --organization=${ORG_ID} \
  --title="Default Access Policy"

# Step 2: Create service perimeter for Cloud Functions
POLICY_ID=$(gcloud access-context-manager policies list --organization=${ORG_ID} --format="value(name)")

gcloud access-context-manager perimeters create ${PERIMETER_NAME} \
  --policy=${POLICY_ID} \
  --title="Cloud Functions Security Perimeter" \
  --resources=projects/${PROJECT_NUMBER} \
  --restricted-services=cloudfunctions.googleapis.com,storage.googleapis.com,secretmanager.googleapis.com \
  --enable-vpc-accessible-services \
  --vpc-allowed-services=cloudfunctions.googleapis.com,logging.googleapis.com

# Step 3: Enforce Organization Policies to prevent privilege escalation
# Disable service account key creation
cat > disable-sa-keys.yaml << EOF
name: projects/${PROJECT_ID}/policies/iam.disableServiceAccountKeyCreation
spec:
  rules:
    - enforce: true
EOF

gcloud org-policies set-policy disable-sa-keys.yaml

# Restrict service account impersonation
cat > restrict-sa-impersonation.yaml << EOF
name: projects/${PROJECT_ID}/policies/iam.allowServiceAccountCredentialLifetimeExtension
spec:
  rules:
    - enforce: false
EOF

gcloud org-policies set-policy restrict-sa-impersonation.yaml

# Enforce domain-restricted sharing
cat > domain-restriction.yaml << EOF
name: projects/${PROJECT_ID}/policies/iam.allowedPolicyMemberDomains
spec:
  rules:
    - allowAll: false
      values:
        allowedValues:
          - "C0XXXXXXX"  # Your organization's customer ID
EOF

gcloud org-policies set-policy domain-restriction.yaml

# Step 4: Disable default service account creation for new functions
cat > disable-default-sa.yaml << EOF
name: projects/${PROJECT_ID}/policies/iam.automaticIamGrantsForDefaultServiceAccounts
spec:
  rules:
    - enforce: true
EOF

gcloud org-policies set-policy disable-default-sa.yaml

# Step 5: Restrict metadata service access in function code (Node.js example)
# Add to function package.json dependencies:
# "@google-cloud/compute-metadata-server": "^1.0.0"

# In function code, validate metadata requests:
cat > metadata-validation.js << 'EOF'
const metadata = require('gcp-metadata');

// Disable metadata service access entirely (recommended)
process.env.GCE_METADATA_HOST = '127.0.0.1:1';

// OR implement strict metadata access controls
const ALLOWED_METADATA_PATHS = [
  '/computeMetadata/v1/project/project-id',
  '/computeMetadata/v1/instance/region'
];

function getMetadata(path) {
  if (!ALLOWED_METADATA_PATHS.includes(path)) {
    throw new Error(`Metadata access denied: ${path}`);
  }
  return metadata.instance(path);
}
EOF

# Verify Organization Policies
echo "\n=== Active Organization Policies ==="
gcloud org-policies list --project=${PROJECT_ID}

nist_csf: PR.PT-4 - Communications and control networks are protected
validation_steps: Verify VPC Service Controls perimeter is active: gcloud access-context-manager perimeters list
Test that functions cannot access restricted services outside perimeter
Confirm Organization Policies are enforced: gcloud org-policies describe POLICY_NAME
Validate service account key creation is blocked
Test metadata service access from function (should be restricted)

mitigations.title: Implement Workload Identity Federation and Short-Lived Tokens
mitigations.description: Replace long-lived service account keys with Workload Identity Federation for external authentication and enforce short-lived access tokens with automatic rotation. Use IAM Conditions for time-based and attribute-based access control.
iso27001_controls: A.9.2.4 - Management of secret authentication information
nist_csf: PR.AC-1 - Identities and credentials are managed
PR.PT-3 - Access to systems and assets is controlled
validation_steps: Verify Workload Identity Pool is configured: gcloud iam workload-identity-pools list
Confirm no service account keys exist: gcloud iam service-accounts keys list --iam-account=SA_EMAIL
Test function can access secrets using Workload Identity
Validate IAM conditions are enforced (test time-based restrictions)
Monitor Cloud Audit Logs for token exchange events: gcloud logging read 'protoPayload.methodName="GenerateAccessToken"'

mitigations.title: Deploy Secret Manager with Least-Privilege Access and Automatic Rotation
mitigations.description: Store all sensitive credentials in Secret Manager with version control, automatic rotation, and IAM conditions restricting access to specific function service accounts. Prevent credential extraction via environment variables or metadata service.

code_example.code:
#!/bin/bash
# Secure Secret Manager configuration with automatic rotation
PROJECT_ID="your-project-id"
SECRET_NAME="database-password"
FUNCTION_SA="fn-process-orders@${PROJECT_ID}.iam.gserviceaccount.com"

# Step 1: Create secret with automatic replication
gcloud secrets create ${SECRET_NAME} \
  --replication-policy="automatic" \
  --labels="environment=production,managed-by=terraform"

# Step 2: Add secret version
echo -n "super-secure-password-$(openssl rand -hex 16)" | \
  gcloud secrets versions add ${SECRET_NAME} --data-file=-

# Step 3: Grant least-privilege access ONLY to specific function SA
gcloud secrets add-iam-policy-binding ${SECRET_NAME} \
  --member="serviceAccount:${FUNCTION_SA}" \
  --role="roles/secretmanager.secretAccessor" \
  --condition="expression=resource.name.startsWith('projects/${PROJECT_ID}/secrets/${SECRET_NAME}') && request.time < timestamp('2026-12-31T23:59:59Z'),title=Time-based access restriction,description=Secret access expires on 2026-12-31"

# Step 4: Configure automatic secret rotation using Cloud Scheduler
cat > rotate-secret.sh << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
# This script runs via Cloud Scheduler to rotate secrets
SECRET_NAME="database-password"
PROJECT_ID=$(gcloud config get-value project)

# Generate new password
NEW_PASSWORD=$(openssl rand -base64 32)

# Add new secret version
echo -n "${NEW_PASSWORD}" | gcloud secrets versions add ${SECRET_NAME} --data-file=-

# Update database password (example using Cloud SQL)
DB_INSTANCE="production-db"
gcloud sql users set-password dbuser \
  --instance=${DB_INSTANCE} \
  --password="${NEW_PASSWORD}"

# Disable old secret versions (keep last 3 versions)
VERSIONS=$(gcloud secrets versions list ${SECRET_NAME} --format="value(name)" | sort -rn | tail -n +4)
for VERSION in $VERSIONS; do
  gcloud secrets versions disable ${VERSION} --secret=${SECRET_NAME}
done

echo "Secret rotation completed: $(date)"
EOF

chmod +x rotate-secret.sh

# Deploy rotation function
gcloud functions deploy rotate-secrets \
  --gen2 \
  --runtime=python311 \
  --region=us-central1 \
  --entry-point=rotate_secret \
  --trigger-topic=secret-rotation-topic \
  --service-account=secret-rotator@${PROJECT_ID}.iam.gserviceaccount.com

# Create Cloud Scheduler job for automatic rotation (weekly)
gcloud scheduler jobs create pubsub rotate-secrets-weekly \
  --location=us-central1 \
  --schedule="0 2 * * 0" \
  --topic=secret-rotation-topic \
  --message-body='{"secret_name":"database-password"}' \
  --time-zone="America/New_York"

# Step 5: Audit secret access in Cloud Audit Logs
cat > secret-access-query.txt << 'EOF'
resource.type="audited_resource"
protoPayload.serviceName="secretmanager.googleapis.com"
protoPayload.methodName="AccessSecretVersion"
protoPayload.resourceName=~"projects/.*/secrets/database-password/versions/.*"
EOF

# Create log-based metric for unauthorized secret access
gcloud logging metrics create unauthorized-secret-access \
  --description="Alerts on secret access from unauthorized service accounts" \
  --log-filter="$(cat secret-access-query.txt) AND NOT protoPayload.authenticationInfo.principalEmail:${FUNCTION_SA}"

# Create alerting policy
gcloud alpha monitoring policies create \
  --notification-channels=CHANNEL_ID \
  --display-name="Unauthorized Secret Access Alert" \
  --condition-display-name="Secret accessed by unauthorized SA" \
  --condition-threshold-value=1 \
  --condition-threshold-duration=60s \
  --condition-filter='metric.type="logging.googleapis.com/user/unauthorized-secret-access"'

# Step 6: Verify secret configuration
echo "\n=== Secret Manager Configuration ==="
gcloud secrets describe ${SECRET_NAME}

echo "\n=== Secret IAM Policy ==="
gcloud secrets get-iam-policy ${SECRET_NAME}

echo "\n=== Active Secret Versions ==="
gcloud secrets versions list ${SECRET_NAME} --filter="state:ENABLED"

tools.name: Secret Manager
tools.cost: $0.06 per 10,000 operations + $0.03 per active secret version

tools.name: Cloud Scheduler
tools.cost: $0.10 per job per month (3 jobs free)

iso27001_controls: A.9.2.4 - Management of secret authentication information
nist_csf: PR.AC-1 - Identities and credentials are managed
validation_steps: Verify secret exists and is enabled: gcloud secrets describe SECRET_NAME
Confirm only authorized service accounts have access: gcloud secrets get-iam-policy SECRET_NAME
Test secret access from Cloud Function works correctly
Validate automatic rotation is scheduled: gcloud scheduler jobs list
Review Cloud Audit Logs for secret access events
Test that old secret versions are automatically disabled after rotation

indicators: Unusual metadata service requests from Cloud Functions to /computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/token
Cloud Function deployment with cloudfunctions.functions.setIamPolicy permission usage
Service account key creation (iam.serviceAccountKeys.create) from Cloud Function IP ranges
Cross-project API calls from Cloud Functions service accounts
Secret Manager access (secretmanager.versions.access) from unexpected Cloud Function revisions
IAM policy modifications (setIamPolicy) with Cloud Functions service account as principal
Privilege escalation attempts: resourcemanager.projects.setIamPolicy calls from function SAs
Lateral movement: Cloud Storage bucket enumeration (storage.buckets.list) from Cloud Functions
Service account impersonation (iam.serviceAccounts.getAccessToken) originating from function execution

log_queries.query:
# Detect Cloud Functions service account privilege escalation attempts
resource.type="cloud_function"
protoPayload.serviceName="cloudfunctions.googleapis.com"
(
  protoPayload.methodName="google.cloud.functions.v1.CloudFunctionsService.SetIamPolicy" OR
  protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.CreateServiceAccountKey" OR
  protoPayload.methodName="google.cloud.resourcemanager.v1.Projects.SetIamPolicy"
)
protoPayload.authenticationInfo.principalEmail=~".*-compute@developer.gserviceaccount.com"
severity>=WARNING

// Detect GCP Cloud Functions privilege escalation (via Azure Sentinel GCP connector)
GCPAuditLogs
| where ServiceName == "cloudfunctions.googleapis.com" or ServiceName == "iam.googleapis.com"
| where MethodName in (
    "SetIamPolicy",
    "CreateServiceAccountKey",
    "GenerateAccessToken",
    "projects.setIamPolicy"
  )
| where PrincipalEmail endswith "-compute@developer.gserviceaccount.com"
| extend 
    SeverityLevel = case(
      MethodName == "projects.setIamPolicy", "Critical",
      MethodName == "CreateServiceAccountKey", "High",
      "Medium"
    )
| summarize 
    EventCount = count(),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated),
    AffectedResources = make_set(ResourceName)
    by PrincipalEmail, MethodName, SeverityLevel
| where EventCount > 3
| order by SeverityLevel desc, EventCount desc

index=gcp_audit sourcetype=google:gcp:pubsub:message
(protoPayload.serviceName="cloudfunctions.googleapis.com" OR protoPayload.serviceName="iam.googleapis.com")
(protoPayload.methodName="SetIamPolicy" OR 
 protoPayload.methodName="CreateServiceAccountKey" OR 
 protoPayload.methodName="GenerateAccessToken" OR
 protoPayload.methodName="projects.setIamPolicy")
protoPayload.authenticationInfo.principalEmail="*-compute@developer.gserviceaccount.com"
| eval severity=case(
    protoPayload.methodName="projects.setIamPolicy", "CRITICAL",
    protoPayload.methodName="CreateServiceAccountKey", "HIGH",
    1=1, "MEDIUM"
  )
| stats count as event_count, 
        earliest(_time) as first_seen, 
        latest(_time) as last_seen,
        values(protoPayload.resourceName) as affected_resources
        by protoPayload.authenticationInfo.principalEmail, protoPayload.methodName, severity
| where event_count > 3
| sort -severity, -event_count
| table first_seen, last_seen, protoPayload.authenticationInfo.principalEmail, protoPayload.methodName, severity, event_count, affected_resources

event.dataset: "gcp.audit" AND 
(
  event.action: ("SetIamPolicy" OR "CreateServiceAccountKey" OR "GenerateAccessToken" OR "projects.setIamPolicy") AND
  gcp.audit.authentication_info.principal_email: *-compute@developer.gserviceaccount.com
) AND
(
  gcp.audit.service_name: "cloudfunctions.googleapis.com" OR 
  gcp.audit.service_name: "iam.googleapis.com" OR
  gcp.audit.service_name: "cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com"
)

log_queries.siem: GCP Cloud Logging - Metadata Service Token Theft
log_queries.query:
# Detect suspicious metadata service token extraction from Cloud Functions
resource.type="cloud_function"
jsonPayload.message=~".*metadata\.google\.internal.*"
jsonPayload.message=~".*/computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/token.*"
severity>=WARNING
| OR
resource.type="http_load_balancer"
httpRequest.requestUrl=~"http://metadata\.google\.internal/.*"
httpRequest.requestUrl=~".*/service-accounts/default/token.*"

log_queries.siem: GCP Cloud Logging - Secret Manager Unauthorized Access
log_queries.query:
# Detect unauthorized Secret Manager access from Cloud Functions
resource.type="audited_resource"
protoPayload.serviceName="secretmanager.googleapis.com"
protoPayload.methodName="AccessSecretVersion"
NOT protoPayload.authenticationInfo.principalEmail=~"fn-.*@.*.iam.gserviceaccount.com"
protoPayload.authenticationInfo.principalEmail=~".*-compute@developer.gserviceaccount.com"
severity>=WARNING

tools.name: GCP Security Command Center
tools.usage: Automatically detects IAM misconfigurations, privilege escalation, and service account abuse

tools.name: Forseti Security
tools.usage: forseti inventory create && forseti scanner run --scanner_name iam_rules_scanner

NIST 800-53 (AC-6 - Least Privilege, CM-5 - Access Restrictions)
